reasoning: Robert Lewandowski currently leads Kylian Mbappé 25 to 22 in the La Liga top scorers race following Matchday 32 (April 19-20), leaving just 6 La Liga matches remaining. Both players have recently suffered injuries: Mbappé sustained a mild ankle sprain on April 16 and Lewandowski suffered a hamstring injury on April 19 that will keep him sidelined for 2-3 weeks. Because the impending Copa del Rey final on April 26 pauses La Liga commitments for both Real Madrid and Barcelona, the overlap between Lewandowski's injury and the league schedule is minimized (he may only miss 1 or 2 league matches). Mbappé has a 3-goal deficit to make up in about 5-6 matches. While he has been in excellent goalscoring form in 2025 (scoring 12 league goals since January), overcoming a 3-goal disadvantage in such a short window is difficult, especially since Mbappé will also have to find his rhythm returning from his ankle injury. Thus, Lewandowski remains the clear favorite, though Mbappé's chances are slightly buoyed by Lewandowski's muscle injury.
